data.title: These financial stocks are trading in overbought territory as earnings season picks up
data.description:
Financial stocks are in focus as second-quarter earnings season gains momentum, with investors assessing whether strong recent rallies across banks, insurers and fintech companies can continue.

Several names in the sector are now trading with elevated Relative Strength Index readings, signaling robust price momentum ahead of a busy slate of earnings releases and management updates.

Wilson Bank Holding Company (WBHC) tops the list with a perfect RSI of 100, reflecting exceptional recent price strength. Oicintra, Inc. (OICT) and NCR Atleos Corporation (NATL) follow with RSI readings of 78 and 77, respectively, while First BanCorp. (FBP) and The Travelers Companies, Inc. (TRV) round out the top five with RSIs of 76.

Other notable names include Columbia Financial, Inc. (CLBK), Globe Life Inc. (GL), PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL), Payoneer Global Inc. (PAYO) and Cullen/Frost Bankers, Inc. (CFR), all posting RSI readings of 70 or higher.

The Relative Strength Index is a widely used momentum indicator that measures the pace and magnitude of recent price moves on a scale of 0 to 100. Readings above 70 typically suggest a stock has entered overbought territory following a sustained rally, while readings below 30 indicate oversold conditions.

Although a high RSI does not necessarily signal an imminent reversal, investors often monitor these stocks closely during earnings season, when financial results and forward guidance can either reinforce bullish momentum or spark profit-taking.

Here is the list:

  1. Wilson Bank Holding Company (WBHC), RSI: 100

  2. Oicintra, Inc. (OICT), RSI: 78

  3. NCR Atleos Corporation (NATL), RSI: 77

  4. First BanCorp. (FBP), RSI: 76

  5. The Travelers Companies, Inc. (TRV), RSI: 76

  6. Columbia Financial, Inc. (CLBK), RSI: 76

  7. Globe Life Inc. (GL), RSI: 75

  8. PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL), RSI: 74

  9. Payoneer Global Inc. (PAYO), RSI: 73

  10. Cullen/Frost Bankers, Inc. (CFR), RSI: 70

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO)

data.title: Syntec Optics joins Russell 3000 Index; shares drop premarket
data.description:
• Syntec Optics Holdings (OPTX) Thursday said that it has been included in the Russell 3000 Index. The final, fully validated list of index members was implemented at the opening of the U.S. equity markets on Monday, June 29, 2026.
  • The company is scheduled to receive education on the Russell indexes on July 24, 2026.
  • The stock is down 4% premarket.

data.title: Cummins updates 2027 engine launch timeline after EPA's proposed rule
data.description:
• Cummins (NYSE: CMI) announced updated plans on Thursday for its Model Year 2027 North American on-highway engine launch after the U.S. EPA proposed changes to upcoming emissions rules.
  • The company will use the proposed regulatory flexibility to gradually roll out its new HELM engine platforms.
  • The company will begin limited production of its new X10 and X15 engines in January 2027, with full production expected by Q3 and Q4 2027, respectively.
  • During the transition, the company will continue producing its current X15, X12, and L9 engines.
  • The company also reaffirmed plans to launch its next-generation B platform in January 2028, while the current B6.7 engine will remain in production through 2027.
  • CEO Jennifer Rumsey said the company's long-term product strategy remains unchanged, adding that the phased rollout is intended to improve the transition while maintaining product quality.
  • The company also noted the production timeline may change once the EPA issues its final rule.
  • On the market side, Cummins shares were trading slightly lower, down around 0.1%, near $659.01 in premarket trading.

data.title: Boeing is said to near FAA approval for 737 Max 10 after years of delays
data.description:
Boeing (BA) is on track to secure final Federal Aviation Administration certification for its long-delayed 737 Max 10 as early as this fall, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the process. The milestone would remove a key obstacle to expanding deliveries of its largest narrowbody jet.

For investors, approval of the Max 10 would complete Boeing's 737 Max lineup and strengthen its position against Airbus in the highly profitable single-aisle aircraft market. The certification would also pave the way for additional deliveries and cash flow as Boeing continues its financial recovery following years of production and regulatory setbacks.

The FAA is expected to certify the Max 10 in September or October, assuming no significant issues emerge before then. The timing remains subject to change as regulators complete the remaining review work.

Certification of the smaller 737 Max 7 is expected first, potentially by late July or early August, according to the news report.

The FAA said it expects to approve both aircraft variants in the coming months but emphasized that safety requirements, rather than a fixed schedule, will determine the timing. 

The Max 7 and Max 10 have faced years of certification delays, preventing Boeing from offering a complete family of 737 Max aircraft. The Max 10, which seats up to 230 passengers, is designed to compete directly with Airbus' popular A321neo.

Earlier this month, Boeing (BA) said flight testing for the Max 10 was about 98% complete, with only two test flights remaining. The company added that certification work is still underway, including completion of required safety analyses and other regulatory reviews before the FAA can grant final approval.

data.title: From IPO pop to sharp drop: SpaceX, a recent case of IPO struggles
data.description:
Apollo’s Torsten Slok has highlighted a persistent pattern of underperformance among IPOs brought to market since 2019. Over the subsequent three years, these newly listed companies have lagged the broader equity market, reflecting structural headwinds rather than temporary setbacks.

Three primary factors explain the weak relative returns. First, the 2020–2021 issuance wave arrived at elevated valuations, fueled by near-zero interest rates, fiscal stimulus, and heightened retail speculation. That left limited upside once conditions normalized. 

Second, the Federal Reserve’s aggressive rate-hiking cycle beginning in 2022 compressed multiples across the board and proved especially punishing for the long-duration, often unprofitable growth names that dominate recent IPO cohorts. 

Third, the boom itself lowered the quality threshold, allowing less mature companies to list while the market benchmark remained dominated by a narrow group of mega-cap leaders that continually raised the performance bar. 

Looking ahead, these pressures show little sign of fading, Slok added. Valuations could again expand in the next issuance window, policy rates appear set to remain structurally higher than in the 2010s, and index leadership continues to concentrate in a handful of large winners—keeping the relative hurdle elevated for new listings. 

The recent SpaceX (SPCX) debut offers a timely case in point. On June 12, the company completed the largest IPO in U.S. history, pricing shares at $135. The stock quickly climbed as high as $225.64—gains of roughly 65%—before reversing course. From that peak just three days after the offering, shares have fallen nearly 50% to trade near $115.19.

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

IPO ETFs: (IPO), (IPOS), (FPXE).

data.title: Google owns $94B in SpaceX shares after historic IPO
data.description:
Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) disclosed in its most recent 10-Q filing that it owned $94B worth of SpaceX (SPCX) shares after the Elon Musk-led company held its historic initial public offering earlier this year. 

The tech giant said $80B of the SpaceX shares are subject to short-term restrictions on the ability to sell, while $14.1B of SpaceX shares are subject to long-term restrictions on the ability to sell through the third quarter of 2027.

SpaceX held its IPO last month, selling 555.5M shares at $135 apiece and raising $75B. When the over-allotment from underwriters was included, SpaceX raised slightly more than $85.7B.

The SpaceX stake helped Google earn $9.11 per share on a GAAP basis during the second quarter, as the tech giant's other income and expense for the quarter ended June 30 was $98B. That included $99B in net gains from equity securities, including SpaceX and a “private company,” widely believed to be Anthropic (ANTHRO).

GAAP rules state that net gains from equity securities, including unrealized gains, need to be included. When stripping that out, Google earned $2.85 per share on an adjusted basis. 

Google shares fell more than 5% in premarket trading on Thursday following its second-quarter results and guidance.

data.title: BeOne expands U.S. footprint with $300M investment in New Jersey
data.description:
BeOne Medicines (ONC) on Thursday announced plans to invest $300M to expand its flagship production facility in Hopewell, New Jersey, bringing its total investments in U.S. manufacturing operations to more than $1B.

As part of the expansion, a new three-story, 145,000-square-foot building, added next to its existing facilities in Hopewell, will house its drug product manufacturing and packaging operations, the Switzerland-based company said.

The expanded site is anticipated to be fully operational in 2029, expanding the total campus area to roughly 545,000 square feet.

BeOne (ONC) expects to create about 120 new full-time jobs from the investment, increasing the total headcount at the site to approximately 240 by the end of 2029.

“Today's announcement reflects BeOne's commitment to strengthening U.S. manufacturing and expanding access to innovative medicines for patients with cancer,” CFO Aaron Rosenberg said.

The investment comes at a time when leading drugmakers such as Lilly (LLY) are rushing to expand their U.S. operations as the Trump administration threatens the pharmaceutical industry with tariffs to boost domestic manufacturing.
